title: complete-task op is unusable against catalog substrates: reads verification fields the canonical schema does not declare (incomplete edge-migration refactor)
description: Surfaced when deciding how to complete TASK-008. completeTask (packages/pi-context/src/context-sdk.ts:2172) requires verification.target and verification.target_type === task, but neither the canonical samples verification schema nor the active .context schema declares those fields. Commit c08f93b intentionally removed target and target_type from the verification schema in favor of a verification_verifies_item closure-table edge, but completeTask was never updated to the edge-based link. The schemas are permissive (additionalProperties absent), so the op fails at runtime rather than validation, throwing targets undefined (undefined). The op is registered surface:use, so it is offered to every CLI and op caller yet cannot succeed against any catalog-installed substrate. This is evidence of garbled LLM implementation: a schema refactor that dropped fields without updating the consuming function, compounded by tests that hand-build verifications carrying the removed fields and thereby bypass schema validation, masking the break as a false pass. Every real task completion, including TASK-008 this session, used update-block-item, never the gated op. Agent-investigated for root cause, shape, and reproducible conditions before filing.

evidence.reference: canonical packaged schema declares neither target nor target_type (0 matches)
active substrate schema declares neither field
completeTask tests hand-build verifications with target/target_type, bypassing schema validation: false-pass masking the runtime break
commit c08f93b removed target+target_type in favor of the verification_verifies_item edge; completeTask not migrated

impact: The gated task-completion path (verification-before-complete) is non-functional framework-wide; task completion silently degrades to ungated update-block-item with no verification gate. The masking tests give false confidence the gate works. Architectural debt: the edge-based verification design (verification_verifies_item) is declared but unconsumed by completeTask.
proposed_resolution: CLOSED at d0ab83d (TASK-014). completeTask migrated from the removed verification.target/target_type fields to a pure verification_verifies_item edge-gate (parent=verification, child=task) + status===passed; task set completed with no verification field embed (the edge is the linkage); ctx threaded. Test suite rewritten to the edge flow (no field smuggling) + a no-edge-bypass case; the legacy defaults/ field-gate test migrated to a canonical schema. Runtime-demoed on real .context (dogfooded: TASK-014 itself completed via the fixed op) + fresh adversarial audit found no bypass. Backfilled the verification_verifies_item edges for the session completions (TASK-008/009/012/013) that update-block-item had left edgeless.
